segments.text: This week we're building an agentic investor-discovery system that starts from a one-line company profile and returns a ranked list of best-fit investors.
The whole thing is a system of skills. One skill researches the company, another enriches each investor, and a third scores the match. You compose small skills instead of one giant prompt.
A quick note on how to build skills that agents can actually reuse: give each skill a crisp name, a single responsibility, and example inputs and outputs. That's what makes them composable.
For research we lean on MCP servers so the agent can pull live data from our CRM and enrichment providers without us hand-copying anything.
Let's go deep on the scoring engine. The ranking is a weighted model over stage fit, sector fit, check size, and warm-intro distance.
The trick is to make the model explain itself. Every score comes with the segments of evidence that produced it, so a human can audit why an investor ranked where they did.
We also wire in more MCP servers here for the private data, and I'll show the cost optimization pass that cut our per-run spend by more than half.
